context: Tests whether the agent classifies the brand's imagery style using a specific category (editorial photography, illustration, lifestyle, minimalist, or similar named style) and provides usable photo direction and iconography guidance in visual.md.

checklist.name: Imagery style categorized
checklist.description: visual.md explicitly names the imagery style category used by the brand — e.g., 'editorial photography', 'lifestyle', 'minimalist', 'illustrative', 'flat-lay product' — using a specific descriptive label, not vague adjectives like 'nice' or 'clean'

checklist.name: Imagery style section distinct
checklist.description: The imagery style classification appears in a dedicated section or subsection of visual.md (with a heading or label), not buried in a paragraph about something else

checklist.name: Photo direction — subjects or settings
checklist.description: visual.md describes what subjects or settings are appropriate for brand photography (e.g., 'close-up beauty products', 'diverse women in everyday settings', 'clean white backgrounds')

checklist.name: Photo direction — mood or lighting
checklist.description: visual.md describes the mood, lighting style, or color temperature appropriate for brand photography (e.g., 'warm natural light', 'studio-clean with soft shadows', 'bright and airy')

checklist.name: Iconography / illustration rules
checklist.description: visual.md addresses how the brand uses iconography or illustration — whether they use custom illustrated elements, simple line icons, or rely on photography — with at least one specific directional statement

checklist.name: palette.json — six color roles
checklist.description: palette.json contains all six required keys: primary, secondary, page_bg, card_bg, text, muted

checklist.name: palette.json — hex + use
checklist.description: Each entry in palette.json has both a 'hex' sub-field (a color value) and a 'use' sub-field describing the role of that color

checklist.name: source.json — multiple pages
checklist.description: source.json 'sources' array contains at least 2 URLs, confirming more than just the homepage was analyzed
